Engineering Intelligence Platform

The intelligence layer beneath your AI coding agents

AI writes great code — it just doesn't know your company. Company Brain gives it your context: your codebase, decisions, and history, so mistakes are caught before code is ever committed.

Start free See how it works

No credit card · Connect a repo in minutes

pull-request #482 · feat/checkout-refactor
// PaymentService.ts
- retryPayment(order, { legacy: true })
+ await paymentGateway.charge(order)
// uses deprecated retry path
// checkout.handler.ts
+ emitEvent('order.placed', order.id)
Company Brain review
Build passes
142 tests green
New event lacks schema
Deprecated retry path — rejected in ADR-31 after the 2024 checkout incident.
Blocked before commit. Suggested fix sent to the agent.
Sits beneath the tools your team already uses
Claude CodeCursorGitHubSlackNotionJiraConfluenceLinear Claude CodeCursorGitHubSlackNotionJiraConfluenceLinear
The idea
Company Brain gives every AI agent your company's context, enforces your standards, remembers past decisions, and stops mistakes before they ship.
01 — The problem

AI writes code. It doesn't know your company.

Today's models are brilliant at syntax and shockingly ignorant of everything that actually makes your codebase yours. So they repeat the same mistakes — and engineers burn hours reviewing, fixing, and re-explaining.

doesn't know
Why past decisions were made
doesn't know
Which architectures were rejected
doesn't know
Your coding standards
doesn't know
Internal conventions
doesn't know
Past production incidents
doesn't know
Existing technical debt
doesn't know
Real business constraints
02 — The solution

A permanent engineering memory for humans and AI

Instead of replacing Claude Code or Cursor, Company Brain sits between the agent and your repository — so every change is checked against how your company actually builds software.

AGENT
AI writes code
Claude Code · Cursor
→
INTELLIGENCE LAYER
Company Brain
Reviews · enforces rules · remembers decisions
→
REPO
Clean, validated code
Only what passed

Everything it learns becomes a structured Engineering Decision Graph — a living model of your architecture, standards, and history that both people and agents can query.

03 — Platform

Everything an agent needs to write code your way

Engineering Decision Graph

A living, structured model of your architecture, standards, and history — the source of truth agents query before they act.

Continuous learning

Ingests GitHub, PRs, ADRs, Slack, Notion, Jira, reviews, incidents, and human corrections — automatically, forever.

Pre-commit review

Every AI-generated change is checked, tested, and validated in the background before a human ever sees it.

Company rule enforcement

Encode conventions and constraints once. Company Brain enforces them on every change, no reminders needed.

Architecture guardrails

Detects violations and deprecated patterns and blocks them, citing the decision or incident that made the rule.

API & MCP server

Connect any agent to your engineering memory over a REST API or MCP server — one context layer for every tool.

04 — The workflow

What happens on every change

Keep scrolling — Company Brain runs the gauntlet step by step. The human only ever sees code that already passed.

01 · Checks build errors
02 · Runs the tests
03 · Detects architecture violations
04 · Verifies company rules
05 · Checks prior decisions
06 · Blocks deprecated patterns
01

Checks build errors

Compiles the change and surfaces breakages instantly.

$ tsc --noEmit → 0 errors
02

Runs the tests

Executes the suite so regressions never slip through.

$ test → 142 passing
03

Detects architecture violations

Flags anything that breaks your intended structure.

guard: no cross-module imports ✓
04

Verifies company rules

Enforces conventions, standards, and constraints.

rules/company.yml → 38 enforced
05

Checks prior decisions

Cross-references the Engineering Decision Graph.

graph: matched ADR-31
06

Blocks deprecated patterns

Stops rejected approaches from creeping back in.

blocked: retryPayment(legacy)
Ships clean, validated code — fixes are sent back to the agent automatically, before anything is committed.
05 — Why it compounds

Context that gets more valuable as you grow

Current AI tools understand programming. Company Brain understands your company — and every decision it captures makes the next one sharper.

Humans see only clean code
Validated, standards-compliant changes — not another review pile.
Fewer review cycles
Mistakes are caught and fixed before the PR is even opened.
Standards enforced automatically
Your conventions apply on every change without anyone policing them.
Memory that compounds
Every decision captured makes the next one faster and safer.
06 — Use cases

Put your engineering memory to work

01
ONBOARDING

Ramp new engineers in days

New hires — and their agents — inherit years of context instantly instead of learning it the hard way.

02
AI GUARDRAILS

Safe autonomy for agents

Let Cursor and Claude Code move fast while Company Brain keeps every change inside your rules.

03
ARCHITECTURE

Preserve hard-won decisions

Rejected approaches and ADRs stay enforced long after the people who made them move on.

04
RELIABILITY

Stop repeat incidents

Once an incident teaches a lesson, that lesson is enforced on every future change automatically.

08 — FAQ

Questions, answered

No — Company Brain sits beneath them. Your agents keep writing code exactly as they do today; we review every change against your company’s context and send fixes back before anything is committed.

Give your AI the context it's been missing

Connect a repository and watch Company Brain start catching what your review process misses.
